world.label: Industrial Revolution begins
world.note: Steam, factories, and cities transform how wealth is produced - the backdrop for classical economics.

world.label: American Revolution / Wealth of Nations
world.note: 'No taxation without representation' - and, the same year, Adam Smith publishes the book that founds modern economics.

world.label: French Revolution
world.note: Fiscal crisis and resentment of taxes like the salt tax (gabelle) help bring down the old order.

world.label: Revolutions of 1848
world.note: Uprisings across Europe; Marx and Engels publish the Communist Manifesto the same year.

world.label: Russian Revolution
world.note: The first attempt to run a large economy by central plan - the real-world test of the calculation debate.

world.label: Great Depression begins
world.note: Mass unemployment that classical theory struggled to explain; the crisis behind Keynes's General Theory.

world.label: Bretton Woods
world.note: Keynes helps design the postwar monetary order: fixed exchange rates, the IMF, and the World Bank.

world.label: Stagflation era
world.note: Inflation and unemployment rise together, breaking the postwar Keynesian consensus and boosting Friedman.

world.label: Fall of the Berlin Wall
world.note: Central planning collapses in Europe; the calculation debate gets its historical verdict.

world.label: Global financial crisis
world.note: A banking collapse revives Keynes, Minsky, and old arguments about markets and regulation.

world.label: COVID-19 pandemic
world.note: Governments spend at wartime scale; supply shocks and inflation revive the monetarist debates.

tax.label: English window tax
tax.note: Tax the countable: windows. People bricked them up - a classic lesson in taxes changing behavior.

tax.label: Pitt's income tax
tax.note: Britain introduces the first modern income tax to fund war against Napoleon.

tax.label: 16th Amendment
tax.note: The U.S. Constitution is amended to allow a federal income tax.

tax.label: Mass income tax & withholding
tax.note: WWII turns income tax from a class tax into a mass tax collected from every paycheck.

tax.label: France invents VAT
tax.note: The value-added tax debuts; it will spread to roughly 175 countries.

tax.label: The supply-side turn
tax.note: Reagan and Thatcher slash top marginal rates, citing Laffer-style arguments.

tax.label: Global minimum corporate tax
tax.note: Over 130 countries agree on a 15% floor (OECD Pillar Two).
